{{Infobox character
| name = Iron King
| appears_in = The Last Horizon
| titles = Iron King, leader
| abilities = channel, augment, and control the powers of others, Mirror of Silence spell, Aether-disrupting magic, psionic pulses
| affiliations = Iron Legion
| introduced = lh-01-the-captain
}}

''See also: [[Iron King Timeline]]''

== Overview ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[The Iron King]] is a powerful entity and the leader of the [[Iron Legion]], a group that spreads through underground factories known as [[Scourge Warrens]] by converting organic and mechanical matter into soldiers. The [[King]] possesses a transcendent intellect and functions as a galaxy-spanning hive mind, granting him virtually limitless resources and the ability to operate on an unprecedented scale. He maintains a deep connection to his [[Legion]], which allows him to enhance and integrate magic through his troops. Physically, the [[King]] is a formidable being with metal features, including a metallic grin and eyes that can burn. His presence is characterized by immense weight, intense heat similar to a furnace, and an overwhelming sense of confidence. Historically, the [[Iron]] [[King]] is a figure of great significance and danger; some have been believed dead only to resurface, and others have been destroyed by being cast into stars. The process of his creation involves complex biological and mechanical integration, often involving exposed organs and circuitry. While he has been described as an ancient masterpiece, his existence is tied to the use of borrowed magic to empower his forces. He has been known to interact with powerful individuals, including Archmages and corporate presidents, sometimes acting as an adversary and other times showing a level of respect for the abilities of those he encounters.}}

== The Captain ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] is a ten-foot-tall, highly intelligent cyborg and the fifth such entity created by the [[Iron Legion]]. After being compressed and forced out of reality by the [[Absolute Burial]] spell, his unborn state was sealed within a torpedo and launched into a star. His current birth is being facilitated by a weapon aboard the [[Iron Hive]], though his consciousness was interrupted during the process. This iteration is linked to the [[Legion]] via a network of veins and wires to an unprecedented degree, allowing him to draw upon the processing power of the entire collective. Currently, he remains strapped to a [[steel]] table with exposed organs and open circuits while his birth continues.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] is a ten-foot-tall fusion of man and machine.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c1">''The Captain'', chapter 1</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] is a being whose birth is being facilitated by a weapon aboard the [[Iron Hive]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c3">''The Captain'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] has led the expansion of the [[Iron Legion]] and killed the narrator in a previous [[life]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] is a fully mature entity that killed the protagonist in their first death.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|A torpedo contains the [[Iron King]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c3" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] was once unborn when the [[Absolute Burial]] spell was used on him.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c8">''The Captain'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] once pierced [[Varic Vallenar]]'s chest with his arm.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c8" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]]'s consciousness was interrupted during the process of birth.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c18">''The Captain'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] was sealed and his body launched into a star.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The [[Legion]] has only managed to create an [[Iron King]]'s mind four times in the past.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The current [[Iron King]] is the fifth one created.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|The current [[Iron King]] is linked more closely to the [[Iron Legion]] than his ancestors.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] is currently strapped to a [[steel]] table with most of his body being open circuits and exposed organs.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c18" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] is a figure within the [[Iron Legion]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c19">''The Captain'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] is the leader associated with the [[Iron Legion]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c20">''The Captain'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] was destroyed by the narrator in the [[Hive]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c21">''The Captain'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|An [[Iron King]] is highly intelligent.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c28">''The Captain'', chapter 28</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] is a masterwork blending of flesh and [[steel]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c28" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] is ten feet tall.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c28" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]]'s eyes are burning portals of magical flame.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c28" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]]'s skull is made of unbreakable glass.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c28" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] is a mage with the signature magic to channel, augment, and control the powers of others.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c28" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] expanded the [[Mirror of Silence]] spell a million times upon receiving it.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c28" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|An [[Iron King]] would want to take [[The Last Horizon]] for his own.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c30">''The Captain'', chapter 30</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|An [[Iron King]] is a high-level entity within the [[Iron Legion]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c31">''The Captain'', chapter 31</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] is an incomprehensible cyborg.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c34">''The Captain'', chapter 34</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] is hooked into the rest of the [[Legion]] via a network of veins and wires.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c34" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]]'s presence is characterized by a ponderous weight and the temperature of a furnace.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c34" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|An [[Iron King]] commanded a fleet that attacked [[The Last Horizon]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c35">''The Captain'', chapter 35</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] is a person who may have provided information to the [[Galactic Union]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c36">''The Captain'', chapter 36</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[Nelthan]] is terrified of the [[Iron King]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c37">''The Captain'', chapter 37</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] is pushing into [[Galactic Union]] territory.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c39">''The Captain'', chapter 39</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] has the processing power of the entire [[Legion]] to draw on.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c39" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]]'s presence is so overwhelming that resistance against him is impossible.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c39" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] once punched through the narrator's chest.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c39" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] is an entity whose [[Grand Hive]] the crew plans to enter on a suicide mission.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c40">''The Captain'', chapter 40</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] has a detection range that the ship must dive past.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c41">''The Captain'', chapter 41</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] possesses fiery eyes and a magnification system built into his existence.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c42">''The Captain'', chapter 42</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] is a figure the narrator feared might seed the system with mines.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c42" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] was caught off guard by the arrival of [[The Last Horizon]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c43">''The Captain'', chapter 43</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] left orders behind to sabotage ships from within using parasites.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c44">''The Captain'', chapter 44</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] has a head much larger than a human's with a brain covered in glass.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c44" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] attempted to twist the [[Aether]]-connection of [[Eurias]] and force compatibility between his magic and the staff's power.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c45">''The Captain'', chapter 45</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] is connected to the [[Legion]] to an unprecedented and theoretically impossible degree.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c45" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] cannot be overwhelmed mentally, physically, or exhausted magically.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c45" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] released [[Eurias]] to fight the [[Divine Titan]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c45" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] is a ten-foot-tall cyborg who weighs two tons.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c46">''The Captain'', chapter 46</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] was compressed to a point and forced out of reality by [[Absolute Burial]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c46" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] can regain freedom through a mental connection to his [[Legion]].<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c47">''The Captain'', chapter 47</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]]'s magic manifests as pink-and-orange flame.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c47" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]]'s hand is a metal skeleton.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c47" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] possesses [[Aether]]-disrupting magic in the form of pink flame.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c47" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]] can send out psionic pulses.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c47" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|[[The Iron King]]'s brain was covered by a glass dome.<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c48">''The Captain'', chapter 48</ref>}}

== The Engineer ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[The Iron King]] is a lethal entity capable of killing people and posed a direct threat to the [[Captain]]'s [[life]]. While rumors circulated that one led the [[Iron Legion]], it was eventually killed by the crew, an act for which [[Sola]] earned Units. This specific figure had previously fought [[Horizon]] and spared the narrator's father, though an [[Iron]] [[King]] in control of the entire [[Legion]] was able to capture [[Mell]]'s father. Although [[Omega]] claimed to have taken over the entity, it is noted that an [[Iron]] [[King]] is not currently ready to be born.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[The Iron King]] is a being that protections can be damaged in battle against.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c3">''The Engineer'', chapter 3</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|An [[Iron King]] was recently destroyed.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c5">''The Engineer'', chapter 5</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|There are rumors of an [[Iron King]] leading the [[Iron Legion]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c8">''The Engineer'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[The Iron King]] was killed by the crew.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c18">''The Engineer'', chapter 18</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Sola]] earned Units for killing the [[Iron King]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c21">''The Engineer'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[The Iron King]] is a being that could kill the [[Captain]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c25">''The Engineer'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Iron]] Kings are entities that can kill people.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c30">''The Engineer'', chapter 30</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[The Iron King]] is not currently ready to be born.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c32">''The Engineer'', chapter 32</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[The Iron King]] is a figure [[Horizon]] fought.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c33">''The Engineer'', chapter 33</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[Omega]] claimed to have taken over the [[Iron King]].<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c36">''The Engineer'', chapter 36</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|[[The Iron King]] spared the narrator's father.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c38">''The Engineer'', chapter 38</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|An [[Iron King]] in control of the entire [[Legion]] was able to capture [[Mell]]'s father.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c41">''The Engineer'', chapter 41</ref>}}

== The Knight ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|Created through the summoning of the [[Iron Legion]] and [[Esh’kinaar]], the [[Iron]] [[King]] is a powerful being who once attempted to seize control of the galaxy. While he can be searched for on the [[Subline]] or located through [[Varic]]'s magic, he remains a formidable enemy capable of delivering heavy blows. During an encounter with [[Benri]], the [[King]] demonstrated his magical scale and expressed a high opinion of [[Benri]]'s own abilities. Despite having been struck by artillery in the past, the [[Iron]] [[King]] was eventually killed, marking him as a target for which no weapon is considered too deadly.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[The Iron King]] can be searched for on the [[Subline]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c11">''The Knight'', chapter 11</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[The Iron King]] is a figure [[Varic]] can attempt to locate using magic.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c16">''The Knight'', chapter 16</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[The Iron King]] is a being created by summoning [[Esh’kinaar]] and the [[Iron Legion]].<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c16" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[The Iron King]] made a bid for control of the galaxy.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c19">''The Knight'', chapter 19</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[The Iron King]] showed [[Benri]] the scale of his magic.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c21">''The Knight'', chapter 21</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[The Iron King]] is an enemy capable of hitting hard.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c24">''The Knight'', chapter 24</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[The Iron King]] was killed.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[The Iron King]] is a target that no weapon is too deadly to use against.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c24" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[The Iron King]] is a target that was previously hit with artillery.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c39">''The Knight'', chapter 39</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|The [[Captain]] has dealt with the [[Iron King]] once before.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c45">''The Knight'', chapter 45</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|[[The Iron King]] holds a high opinion of [[Benri]]'s magic.<ref name="lh-03-the-knight-c45" />}}

== The Pilot ==
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[The Iron King]] is a mysterious, apocalyptic entity characterized by a metal smile and a flickering, spark-like visible brain. This being previously forced [[Benri Vallenar]] into a magical contract and later engaged in a one-on-one conversation with him, though it was not responsible for the death of the [[Captain]]'s father. While a previous version of the entity was completely destroyed, research found in a data core reveals that [[the Perfected]] were attempting to create such beings. Although the [[Iron Legion]] currently lacks a leader to coordinate its [[Hives]], an [[Iron]] [[King]] has appeared for the first time in centuries, marking it as the final one in the galaxy. Now contained within an [[Aethertech box]], the entity can be granted permission for use by [[Benri Vallenar]] and has declared that its game is not yet over.}}

{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[The Iron King]] is a figure mentioned in relation to [[Benri]]'s desire for guards.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c2">''The Pilot'', chapter 2</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[The Iron King]] is a mysterious entity that previously made [[Benri]] sign a magical contract.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c2" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[The Iron King]] did not kill the [[Captain]]'s father.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c4">''The Pilot'', chapter 4</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[The Iron King]] was completely destroyed.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c8">''The Pilot'', chapter 8</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The narrator has been afraid of something since their battle with the [[Iron King]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c10">''The Pilot'', chapter 10</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[The Perfected]] were attempting to create an [[Iron King]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c20">''The Pilot'', chapter 20</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[The Iron King]] was the final [[Iron King]] in the galaxy.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c22">''The Pilot'', chapter 22</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|An [[Iron King]] has appeared for the first time in centuries.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c23">''The Pilot'', chapter 23</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The data core contains research regarding the creation of the [[Iron King]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c25">''The Pilot'', chapter 25</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[The Iron King]] is an apocalyptic enemy who has brought the narrator to the brink of death.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c26">''The Pilot'', chapter 26</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[Sola]]'s crew includes people who can duel an [[Iron King]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c32">''The Pilot'', chapter 32</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[The Iron King]] has a metal smile and visible brain that flickers like sparks of light.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c38">''The Pilot'', chapter 38</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[The Iron King]] stated that the game has not ended yet.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c38" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The [[Iron Legion]] lacks an [[Iron King]] to coordinate its [[Hives]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c39">''The Pilot'', chapter 39</ref>}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[The Iron King]] is a figure [[Benri Vallenar]] has had a one-on-one conversation with.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c39" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[The Iron King]] is contained within an [[Aethertech box]].<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c39" />}}
{{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|[[The Iron King]] is a being that [[Benri Vallenar]] can give permission to use.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c39" />}}

== Notable quotes ==
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|“Benri Vallenar. We are here to extend an invitation on behalf of the Iron King.”<ref name="lh-01-the-captain-c20" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|“If they did have a King, as rumors suggested, they must have lost him.<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c8" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|“First I got to take over the Iron King, and now you?”<ref name="lh-02-the-engineer-c36" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|“I’m on a crew with people who can duel an Iron King.”<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c32" />}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|The Iron King said, “It seems our game hasn’t ended quite yet.<ref name="lh-04-the-pilot-c38" />}}

== Notes ==
<references />

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:The Captain]]
[[Category:The Engineer]]
[[Category:The Knight]]
[[Category:The Pilot]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon]]
[[Category:The Last Horizon characters]]
[[Category:Iron Legion]]
[[Category:Titled characters]]
[[Category:Mages]]
